home.aboutBody1: This website shows six climate indicators for Norway: CO₂ emissions, surface temperature anomaly, projected sea level rise, historical precipitation, glacier retreat, and current air quality. All data comes from open scientific sources.
home.aboutBody2: The numbers are best understood as approximations. Temperature and precipitation come from reanalysis models (NASA GISS and ERA5), which blend observations with atmospheric modelling rather than reading directly from sensors. Sea level projections follow IPCC AR6 global medians and do not account for local land movement or regional ocean variation. CO₂ figures are national totals. Glacier outlines are survey snapshots, not continuous measurements, and coverage varies between periods.
home.aboutBody3: This is a portfolio project, not a scientific tool. For authoritative climate data, consult the Norwegian Environment Agency or the Norwegian Meteorological Institute.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you are looking for does not exist.

co2.title: CO₂ Emissions
co2.sliderHelp:
Drag the slider to explore Norway’s CO₂ emissions year by year. Darker shades mean higher emissions.

 Press play to animate through the years automatically.
co2.sliderAriaLabel: Select year for CO₂ emissions data
co2.chartAriaLabel: Line chart of Norway’s CO₂ emissions over time

temperature.title: Temperature Anomaly
temperature.description: Surface temperature anomaly relative to 1951–1980 baseline
temperature.sliderHelp:
Drag the slider to explore Norway’s surface temperature anomaly year by year. Red means warmer than average, blue means cooler. 

 Press play to animate through the years automatically.
temperature.sliderAriaLabel: Select year for temperature anomaly data
temperature.chartAriaLabel: Line chart of Norway’s average temperature anomaly over time

sealevel.title: Sea Level Rise
sealevel.description: Projected global sea level rise by emissions scenario (IPCC AR6)
sealevel.sliderHelp:
Drag the slider to explore projected global sea level rise every 10 year (2020-2100), including Norway and coastlines worldwide. The blue overlay shows land areas that would fall below the rising sea level.

Zoom in for more detail along coastlines. Switch between emissions scenarios to compare outcomes.

Projections are global median estimates (IPCC AR6). Local sea level may vary and coastal elevation accuracy is limited, so treat this as an approximation.

 Press play to animate through the decades automatically.
sealevel.sliderAriaLabel: Select year for sea level projection
sealevel.chartAriaLabel: Line chart of projected global sea level rise by scenario

precipitation.title: Precipitation
precipitation.description: Historical annual precipitation totals for Norway (ERA5, 1940-2024)
precipitation.sliderHelp:
Drag the slider to explore annual precipitation totals across Norway year by year. Darker blue means more rainfall.

Data covers 1940 to 2024 from the ERA5 reanalysis dataset. Values show average mm per year across grid cells clipped to Norway.

 Press play to animate through the years automatically.
precipitation.chartAriaLabel: Line chart of Norway average annual precipitation over time

glacier.title: Glacier Retreat
glacier.sliderHelp:
Select a survey period to see glacier extents across Norway and zoom in to see glaciers. Each polygon is a mapped glacier. Total area has fallen from 3,350 km² in 1947-1985 to 2,328 km² in 2018-2019.

Note: the 1988-1997 and 1999-2006 surveys only covered larger glaciers so their totals are not directly comparable with the comprehensive inventories.

 Press play to step through all periods automatically.

 Click any glacier polygon to see its name and area.
glacier.chartAriaLabel: Bar chart of total mapped glacier area per survey period

airquality.title: Air Quality
airquality.description: Current air quality forecast for Norwegian monitoring stations (met.no)
airquality.help:
This map shows air quality monitoring stations across Norway. Data comes from the Norwegian Meteorological Institute's forecast model, updated daily.

Click any station (purple dot) to see the current Air Quality Index and pollutant concentrations: NO₂, PM2.5, PM10, and ozone (O₃).

AQI scale: Low (1-2), Moderate (2-3), High (3-4), Very high (4+).
airquality.clickHint: Click a station to view air quality
